item.title: Cisco Splunk Add-on for AppDynamics - v3.1.9 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

bug fix, error adding new controllers


Description:
The Splunk Add-on for AppDynamics enables you to configure data inputs to collect and correlate data from Splunk AppDynamics REST APIs. Splunk AppDynamics is an application performance monitoring platform for hybrid and on-premises applications. The add-on provides modular inputs to collect data on application performance, business transaction performance, infrastructure performance, and health rule violations from Splunk AppDynamics. Use the add-on to correlate Splunk AppDynamics data with your other machine data (such as logs, wire data, server data, and infrastructure data) in the Splunk platform. The add-on also provides dashboards to monitor and troubleshoot add-on performance. The Splunk Add-on for AppDynamics contains seven input types: High Level Status Database Metrics Hardware Metrics Analytics Search Secure Application Data Events Data Custom Metrics Application BT Snapshots Controller Audit Records Controller License Usage This TA is required for the Content Pack for Splunk AppDynamics https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CPAppDynamics/latest/CP/About
item.dc:creator: Splunk Works

item.title: InSitzes App for Splunk Enterprise Monitoring - v2.0.14 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Minor Updates And Cleanup


Description:
The InSitzes App for Splunk Enterprise Monitoring provides administrators with actionable insights into ingestion, performance, workload, and search behavior across their Splunk Enterprise environment. By consolidating system, data, and workload visibility into one app, it enables teams to proactively detect issues, optimize resource usage, and improve the overall Splunk Enterprise experience.
item.dc:creator: David Sitzes

item.title: Corelight App For Splunk - v2.5.8 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

  • New Features
  • Dashboards
    • Data Insights
    • Anomaly Detection Details - Added Drilldown Dashboards for Admin_Shares and RDP Use Cases
    • Data Explorer
    • ICS/OT Monitoring - Asset and security insights of ICS/OT protocols
    • Data Aggregation support for Dashboard Studio versions of Connections, DNS, Files, HTTP, and SSL and x509 dashboards
    • Corelight Menu
    • Operations Insights - Added 3 Visualizations to help Users understand Storage Needed when enabling SmartPCAP
    • External Corelight Resources
      • Links to "Corelight Docs" and "Zeek Cheat Sheet"
  • Improvements
  • Dark theme supported
  • Dashboards
    • Anomaly Detection Details Dashboards - Optimized experience for existing 5 Use Cases
    • Corelight Suricata IDS Alert Overview
    • Replaced "Category Analysis" Table with a Sankey
    • Added description for "Signature Analysis" to click on SID
    • In "Log Details" Panel, if has_payload field returns yes, Users may click on the session_id to view the Decoded Payload
    • Alert Aggregations
    • Alerts Table now filters based on Impact and Category Input Filter selections.
    • Secure Channel Insights
    • Added missing traffic direction and IP classification fields to DNS and SSL sourcetypes.
    • Removed 8 Unused Visualizations and related 5 broken Datasources
    • Software
    • Converted dashboard from Classic XML to Dashboard Studio


Description:
The Corelight App for Splunk enables incident responders and threat hunters who use Splunk® and Splunk Enterprise Security to work faster and more effectively. The app and required TA extracts information and knowledge from Zeek (formerly known as Bro) via Corelight Sensors or open-source Zeek, resulting in powerful security insights through key traffic dashboards such as: Intel: Find IOCs from external sources matched in network traffic. Notices: See situations flagged by the Notice policy for further investigation. IP Interrogation: Identify anomalies by reviewing top protocol usage, internal vs. external connections, top connections by bytes transferred and more. Log Hunting: Accelerate your hunt by narrowing down many logs to only the logs that matter. Detections: Find and respond to off-port protocol usage, IOC matches, and other potentially interesting events. Connections: Gain situational awareness using lists of top services, ports, dataflows, originators, and responders. HTTP: Find suspicious HTTP transactions by reviewing a list of top host headers, originators, rare user agents and rare host headers. DNS: Detect DNS exfiltration by spotting queries to non-existent domains and high connection counts. Files: Find executables hidden in benign extensions and compressed files. Corelight Egress Monitor: Find risky North/South user connections to weak SSL versions.
item.dc:creator: Corelight Inc

item.title: TA for Corelight - v2.5.8 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Please see https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3884 for details.


Description:
This is the Indexer TA for the Corelight App.
item.dc:creator: Corelight Inc

item.title: 3D Graph Network Topology Visualization - v1.4.2 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Fixed security vulnerabilities on dependencies


Description:
Graph Algorithms for Machine Learning Toolkit and custom visualisation for Splunk to plot relationships between objects with force directed graph based on ThreeJS/WebGL. Find more information on the details page with link to blog articles like: https://www.splunk.com/en_us/blog/machine-learning/chasing-a-hidden-gem-graph-analytics-with-splunk-s-machine-learning-toolkit.html
item.dc:creator: Splunk Works

item.title: Druva App For Splunk - v1.2.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Version: 1.2.0

  • Unified "All Events" Dashboard: A new "All Events" page offers a centralized location for viewing all alerts and events, streamlining your monitoring process.
  • Comprehensive Event Coverage: The App now includes and displays Administrator Events alongside existing alerts, offering better insight into system-wide activities and operational updates.


Description:
The Druva App for Splunk integrates the event data visibility of the Druva Cloud Platform with Splunk's powerful investigation and visualization tools, offering advanced reporting and analysis capabilities. This app empowers backup administrators, security analysts, and IT operations teams to correlate data backup and restore events across the infrastructure, both in real-time and historically. It also provides valuable insights into key performance indicators related to both operational and security aspects, based on the data received. By automating complex incident analysis that once required days of manual, error-prone data mining, the app saves time and resources, allowing IT teams to focus on resolving critical issues. Key Features of Druva's Ransomware Recovery Solution Data Integrity: Air-gapped, immutable backups ensure ransomware can’t execute in the Druva environment. You will always have safe backup data for recovery, even in the event of an attack. Operational Security: Enjoy 24x7x365 fully managed security operations, including automatic patching and continuous monitoring to detect threats early. Accelerated Recovery: The integration with Splunk platforms enables faster threat detection and response, coupled with Druva’s Accelerated Recovery solutions to help you return to normal operations more quickly. Benefits By integrating Druva Cloud Platform with your existing Splunk platform, this solution delivers real-time monitoring of backup systems, detecting unusual activities such as unauthorized access, data modification, or failed backup attempts. This unified approach empowers your security and IT teams to act quickly, respond to threats in real-time, and ensure business continuity—especially when faced with complex ransomware or data loss incidents. To learn how Druva’s comprehensive integration ecosystem can strengthen cyber resilience, visit the Druva website.
item.dc:creator: Tech Partners

item.title: Druva Add-on For Splunk - v2.4.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Version: 2.4.0

  • Druva Add-on for Splunk 2.4.0 adds Event V3 API support to fetch events and display them on the All Events page.
  • Configuration complexity is reduced by eliminating the need to add separate accounts in the Druva Add-on for Splunk App to receive event updates for Endpoints/SaaS Apps and Enterprise workloads.
  • Event ingestion timestamps are corrected and epoch-to-UTC conversion is fixed so event times are accurate in Splunk


Description:
The Druva Add-on for Splunk enables Splunk Enterprise administrators to easily ingest data from the Druva Cloud Platform into Splunk for comprehensive monitoring and analysis. This integration empowers you to consume Druva-related data through the Druva App for Splunk or any custom apps created for your Security Operations Center (SOC) or IT operations. Key Features of Druva's Ransomware Recovery Solution Data Integrity: Air-gapped, immutable backups ensure ransomware can’t execute in the Druva environment. You will always have safe backup data for recovery, even in the event of an attack. Operational Security: Enjoy 24x7x365 fully managed security operations, including automatic patching and continuous monitoring to detect threats early. Accelerated Recovery: The integration with Splunk platforms enables faster threat detection and response, coupled with Druva’s Accelerated Recovery solutions to help you return to normal operations more quickly. What This Integration Pack Does This two-way API integration connects the Druva Cloud Platform to your Splunk platform, enabling automated response actions and streamlined recovery. Key capabilities include: Automated Response Actions: Quarantine affected resources or snapshots to halt the spread of ransomware, reducing the risk of reinfection or contamination. Recovery Initiation: Restore endpoints or data to a secure state, rolling back to a point in time before the attack occurred. Remote Resource Wiping: Effortlessly delete quarantined snapshots and wipe compromised resources to minimize the impact of malware. Malicious Hash Search: Quickly identify and remediate malicious content within backup data to enhance threat mitigation efforts. Benefits By integrating Druva Cloud Platform with your existing Splunk platform, this solution delivers real-time monitoring of backup systems, detecting unusual activities such as unauthorized access, data modification, or failed backup attempts. This unified approach empowers your security and IT teams to act quickly, respond to threats in real-time, and ensure business continuity—especially when faced with complex ransomware or data loss incidents. To learn how Druva’s comprehensive integration ecosystem can strengthen cyber resilience, visit the Druva website.
item.dc:creator: Tech Partners

item.title: Gotify Alert Action - v2.0.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
Adds an alert action to Splunk that sends a message to a Gotify server.
item.dc:creator: Michael Clayfield

item.title: Apprise Alert Action - v2.0.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
Adds an alert action to Splunk that allows sending a notification using any of the notification services supported by Apprise (Slack, Office 365, syslog, Telegram, Signal, webhook, email + many, many more). For a full list of supported services see https://github.com/caronc/apprise/wiki
item.dc:creator: Michael Clayfield

item.title: Guard Detect Add-on for Splunk - v2.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Added outbound HTTPS proxy support with optional Basic authentication for all Guard Detect requests. New Configuration > Proxy settings (URL, username, encrypted password), safe logging (no secrets), and basic URL validation. Documentation updated. Backward compatible; proxy is disabled by default.


Description:
Guard Detect is an intelligent threat detection system designed for Atlassian cloud products. It enables organizations to detect, investigate, and respond to suspicious user activity within Atlassian cloud environments. By offering comprehensive visibility into user activities and data interactions, Guard Detect proactively detects and assists in mitigating potential breaches. The Guard Detect Add-on for Splunk presents a streamlined solution for configuring and ingesting alert data from Guard Detect into Splunk. This integration allows organizations to retrieve and index alerts in near real-time. By leveraging this powerful combination of Guard Detect and Splunk, organizations can gain valuable insight into their security landscape. Once you connect the Add-on for Splunk, alerts will be shared with Splunk.
item.dc:creator: Atlassian Inc

item.title: subzero - v1.2.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

This version of the application adds functionality for windows platforms. Be sure to review and configure the inputs.conf file as well as the myindexes.csv file for your needs. Please ensure the full path of the frozen buckets is set in the myindexes.csv per index and the Splunk home directory is specified in the buckets.ps1 script for windows depending upon where Splunk is installed on the server.


Short Description:
Subzero aims to provide a user interface to quickly identify the age of frozen data so that the administrator can take appropriate actions such as thaw out or delete specific data.


Description:
PROBLEM: Within Splunk architectures that utilize frozen storage of data ingested into Splunk, the data then accumulates until space runs out and mechanisms outside of Splunk itself will need to be used to remain vigilant of the data age and volume. SOLUTION: Subzero aims to provide a user interface to quickly identify the age of frozen data so that the administrator can take appropriate actions such as thaw out or delete specific data.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Bowen

item.title: Cisco Security Cloud - v3.6.2 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Release Notes v3.6.2

Features and Enhancements

  • Duo Logs: Added LOOKAHEAD support to improve log parsing and processing efficiency.
  • Network Visibility Module (NVM): Introduced Process Tree Visualization, enabling interactive exploration of endpoint processes.
  • Endpoint Visibility Module (EVM): Implemented updated mappings to enhance data correlation and visibility.


Description:
The Cisco Security Cloud application offers seamless integration for connecting your Cisco devices with Splunk. It features a modular UX input design, built-in health checks, and constant monitoring to ensure operational integrity. Product(s) Enabled: Cisco AI Defense Cisco Duo Cisco Email Threat Defense (ETD) Cisco Endpoint Visibility Module (EVM) CIM Mappings (Beta) Cisco Identity Intelligence (CII) Cisco Multicloud Defense Cisco NVM Cisco Secure Endpoint Cisco Secure Firewall (FTD/eStreamer/ASA) Cisco Secure Workload Cisco Isovalent Cisco Isovalent Edge Processor **Alpha Cisco Secure Malware Analytics (SMA) Cisco Secure Network Analytics (SNA) Cisco Vulnerability Intelligence Cisco XDR (Incident Import & Promote to ES Notable)
item.dc:creator: Cisco Systems, Inc.

item.title: App Compatibility Checker for Splunk - v1.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Added support for distributed search environments.
Enhanced main dashboard to only load after a variable is selected.


Description:
The App Compatibility Checker identifies installed apps that are not compatible with a selected target Splunk version and displays them on the Incompatible Apps dashboard. It helps administrators: - Prepare for Splunk upgrades - Detect archived or unsupported apps - Reduce upgrade risk The app only covers apps not built by Splunk. To check the compatibility status of Splunk-built apps use Splunk Health Assistant Add-On.
item.dc:creator: Daria Garmash

item.title: Akamai MFA App for Splunk - v2.0.8 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

  • Starting with version 2.0.0 of the Akamai MFA Splunk app, Python 2 is no longer supported.
  • Logs generated by the plugin are now saved in the index: akamai_mfa.
  • The plugin now collects:
  • Session history logs
  • Resource actions
  • The data retention policy is set to 180 days.
  • 30 days of data is now fetched by default.
  • Data is now saved in the index akamai_mfa.
  • New indexes have been added for Session history and Resource actions.
  • Please refer to the documentation for instructions on setting up the new plugin and migrating existing data.


Description:
Akamai MFA is a next-generation MFA solution that features a phishing-resistant push authentication factor utilizing FIDO2. Akamai MFA couples the proven security of FIDO2 with the familiar and frictionless user experience of a mobile push — removing the need for costly and cumbersome hardware security keys. Deployed on the Akamai Intelligent Edge Platform, Akamai MFA can be activated and managed centrally via Enterprise Center with global reach and scale for resilience and performance. The Akamai MFA App for Splunk allows customers to ingest authentication events and other data from the Akamai MFA service into Splunk for further analysis and correlation.
item.dc:creator: Akamai Technologies

item.title: Bitwarden Event Logs - v2.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

  • [PM-26378] add new events (#171)
  • Various dependency updates


Description:
This app provides insight into activity of your Bitwarden organization such as user's activity (logged in, changed password, 2fa, etc.), cipher activity (created, updated, deleted, shared, etc.), collection activity, organization activity, and more.
item.dc:creator: Bitwarden Inc

item.title: ANY.RUN Add-on for Splunk - v1.1.3 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Changed authorization method for the TI Feeds. Please use API Key to authenticate. Bug fix.


Short Description:
The ANY.RUN Add-on for Splunk integrates ANY.RUN Interactive Sandbox, ANY.RUN Threat Intelligence Lookup, and ANY.RUN Threat Intelligence Feeds with Splunk. Get real-time malware analysis, instant IOC enrichment, and threat data import for faster verdicts and proactive detection.


Description:
Integrating ANY.RUN’s solutions with Splunk platform is seamless with the ANY.RUN Add-on for Splunk. Easily connect your Spunk platform with all three flagship products by ANY.RUN – Interactive Sandbox, Threat Intelligence Lookup (TI Lookup), and Threat Intelligence Feeds (TI Feeds) – for faster investigations and response. The app unlocks access to real-time updates, quick verdicts, and actionable statistics directly in Splunk, empowering analysts with automated enrichment and instant visibility into threats. Use cases: – Quick Verification: Analyze suspicious URLs from Spunk events in ANY.RUN Sandbox for rapid verdicts. Analysis results are stored as new Splunk events, with extracted IOCs captured for proactive detection of similar threats. – Instant Enrichment: Enrich IOCs (hashes, IPs, domains, URLs) with threat context via TI Lookup. Tags, industry insights, and linked sandbox analyses will be automatically imported to Splunk. – Real-Time Threat Data Import: Automated delivery of IOCs from TI Feeds to Splunk uncovers latest data on threats investigated by 15,000+ SOC teams worldwide. Benefits: – Faster Decision-Making: Accelerate MTTR with immediate verdicts and actionable insights. – No Blind Spots: Contextualize alerts with fresh threat data for confident decisions. – Verified Data: Near-zero false positives rate to save time and reduce workload. – Deep Visibility: From high-level insights to detailed technical details, the app ensures visibility at all depth levels.
item.dc:creator: ANY.RUN
